subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, logical narrative from introduction through modelling to conclusion
Wellâstructured with descriptive headings, subheadings and labelled figures
Descriptive title immediately orients the reader to the investigationâs focus
weaknesses: Occasional unnecessary detail and repetition reduce conciseness
Long algebraic derivations are embedded rather than summarized
Inconsistent table captions and missing units hinder interpretability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report is coherent and logically developed, with a clear structure supported by headings and figures. However, lengthy narrative passages and overly detailed derivations detract from conciseness and prevent an âexceptionalâ rating. This justifies a solid score of 3 out of 4.

strengths: Consistent use of appropriate calculus and regression notation for the most part
Effective use of multiple representations (tables, GeoGebra/Desmos graphs, derivative plots)
Logical sequence of argument is generally clear
weaknesses: Dense algebraic paragraphs obscure key steps at times
Some graphs lack axis labels and scales, reducing clarity
Occasional notation slips (missing differentials, superscript errors)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication is generally clear, with effective representations and largely correct notation. Minor inconsistencies and dense exposition prevent a top mark but support a robust 3 out of 4.

strengths: Choice of a unique glass and personal narrative demonstrate clear engagement
Evidence of independent thought in deriving bespoke regression methods
Good creativity in combining technology with custom derivations
weaknesses: Some sections revert to textbook exposition rather than personal insight
Decisions sometimes rely heavily on technology without full studentâled justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach, but occasional reliance on standard methods and limited depth of innovation warrant a midâlevel score of 2 out of 3.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on modelling choices and their limitations
Clear evaluation of outcomes, including comparison to manufacturer volume and airâtoâwine ratio
Suggestions for plausible extensions and improvements
weaknesses: Critical analysis remains surface level in places (e.g., volume discrepancy)
Depth of reflection could be enhanced by discussing sensitivity (e.g., z-test p-value effects)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides thoughtful analysis and clear evaluation of outcomes, but the reflection lacks the depth and critical insight required for the highest band, supporting a score of 2 out of 3.

strengths: Mathematics directly targets the aim with effective application of calculus, regression and trigonometry
Work is fully commensurate with SL level, including limits, integration and leastâsquares
Insightful application of oneâsided limits and Riemann sums demonstrates strong understanding
weaknesses: Minor algebraic errors in manual cubic construction
Heavy reliance on technology for some integrals without full manual verification
Goodnessâofâfit metrics (SSE, RÂ²) are not numerically computed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is highly relevant and applied appropriately at SL level, showing thorough understanding despite minor errors. This places the work firmly in band 4, meriting 4 out of 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: While the sequence of arguments is logical, lengthy paragraphs and dense algebra sometimes obscure the line of reasoning; some graphs are inserted without immediate contextual explanation. Overall clarity is acceptable but not consistently âgoodâ, so band 2 fits.

Derivations show good understanding; final numerical results are plausible. Nevertheless, algebraic slips (e.g. mis-copied cubic, notation inconsistencies) and heavy reliance on technology for complex integrals reveal minor inaccuracies, keeping this to âgood understanding with minor errorsâ.

Tables of data, numerous GeoGebra/Desmos graphs, derivative plots and a 3-D solid image provide multiple representations that clarify the argument. They are appropriate and generally well referenced, so the use is âeffectiveâ.

The exploration follows a clear narrative: introduction of context, data collection, modelling of successive intervals, volume calculation, and evaluation. Although lengthy, each section logically follows from the previous and builds towards answering the stated aim. Minor digressions and occasional repetition prevent it from reaching the âhighly coherentâ descriptor.

Employing least-squares derivations, one-sided limits to enforce continuity, and a bespoke normal-distribution attempt demonstrate creativity and initiative beyond routine classwork, yet the techniques stay within known SL territory; thus âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ creativity.

There is thoughtful analysis of residual minimisation, comparison of quadratic vs exponential fits, and discussion of continuity adjustments; however, the critique of results (e.g. significance of 12.5 cmÂ³ discrepancy) remains surface level, hence band 2.

Outcomes are compared to manufacturer volume and an air-to-wine ratio is interpreted. The evaluation section lists plausible extensions, satisfying the descriptor of âclear evaluation of outcomes and implicationsâ.

The student reflects on modelling choices, recognises shortcomings (photograph scaling, discontinuities) and proposes improvements. Reflection recurs in several sections but is not deeply woven throughout, so it meets âmeaningfulâ rather than âsubstantialâ depth.

Calculus notation, piecewise domain statements and parameter symbols are largely correct; occasional slips (missing differentials, superscript errors) appear but do not impede meaning. Hence âmostly consistentâ use rather than fully consistent.

The student collects personal data, debates alternative functions, and inserts personal anecdotes about wine culture, indicating a clear personal approach. However, the narrative occasionally drifts into textbook exposition rather than individual insight, so band 2 is appropriate.

The work is relevant to the aim, yet large passages (trial-and-error narratives, screenshots of failed models, repeated explanations of standard techniques) lower conciseness. The student could have pruned several pages without loss of mathematical substance, hence âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ.

All mathematics directly addresses modelling the outline and volume; calculus, regression, and trigonometric identities are well-connected to the aim. The range is broad and purposeful, so relevance is âeffectiveâ (band 4) rather than merely appropriate.

Choosing an unusual glass, devising piecewise modelling and self-deriving regression formulae show genuine independent thought, yet many decisions rely on technology regressions or qualitative judgements; evidence is âsignificantâ but not outstanding.

Headings, sub-headings and labelled figures give the report an obvious structure (introduction, modelling sections, integration, conclusion, evaluation). Transitions are generally smooth, but some subsections (e.g. long algebraic derivations) feel embedded rather than summarised, so the organisation is âwellâ rather than âexceptionallyâ organised.

Differentiation, definite integration, limits and least-squares procedures are solidly within SL level and are applied across several contexts. Occasional ventures into normal-distribution theory are peripheral but still understandable at SL. Thus mathematics is âfully commensurateâ with course level.

annotations.comment: The clear, descriptive title establishes the investigationâs focus immediately and supports reader orientation.
The personal narrative in the rationale effectively situates the exploration within the studentâs experience, demonstrating genuine engagement.
The expanded normal equations for least squares are derived correctly, but summation notation is dense; consider breaking into numbered steps for clarity.
Your simultaneous-equation solution is well executed; summarizing key formulas in a table could improve logical presentation.
The connection between higher ABV and required air space needs clearer justification or a reference to quantify the relationship rather than general reasoning.
The studentâs choice of a unique glass (Josephine No. 3) illustrates independent thinking and personal motivation for this investigation.
The graph in Figure 4 lacks axis labels and scales, making it difficult to assess fits quantitatively; add labels to improve clarity.
Table 1 and Table 2 should be titled and captioned consistently, and units for x and y must be specified for full interpretability.
Consider explaining why the data interval of 0.5 cm was chosen and how many data points ensure a reliable model outline.
The analysis of the quarticâs concavity is insightful; consider quantifying the curvature error to strengthen the critique.
When defining piecewise intervals by slope sign changes, specify the quantitative slope thresholds used to partition the curve.
The description of concavity misfits in the quartic function shows thoughtful critique; summarise this rather than overly detailed narrative for conciseness.
When proposing both quadratic and exponential fits, include a plan for comparing their goodness of fit numerically (e.g. SSE or RÂ²).
The equation parameters lack differential or function notation consistency; ensure all superscripts and signs are clear (e.g. xÂ³ term).
The narrative description of stretching the parabola would be clearer with annotated equations showing each transformation step-by-step.
Show explicitly the natural-log transformation steps in the linearisation to aid clarity and reproducibility of your regression process.
The matrix solution figure aids understanding, but the handwritten layout is hard to read; typeset the matrix for consistent presentation.
The stepwise derivation of cosine parameters is clear, but residual plots or SSE values would strengthen model selection beyond visual fit.
In the z-test inversion, sensitivity to p-values near 1 should be discussed; mention how small changes affect Ï to justify method limitations.
The use of the standard deviation formula refocuses on reliable statistics; include the computed Ï value and show substitution for transparency.
The manual cubic construction shows initiative but has algebraic errors in both integrand and exponent; re-derive f(x) carefully with correct terms.
The application of one-sided limits to enforce continuity is insightful and demonstrates strong understanding of piecewise function behaviour.
The Riemann-sum derivation for volume is well explained; cite the theorem name and link to classical volume of revolution concept.
The detailed integration steps for the exponential segment are accurate; consider summarizing final expressions to maintain conciseness.
The WolframAlpha-aided cubic volume computation is efficient but should include a screenshot or copy of the integral input for reproducibility.
The concluding evaluation summarizes results effectively; adding a critical discussion of the 12.5 cmÂ³ volume discrepancy would deepen reflection.
